message:
fcp-sdk,fcp-store: reject tampered connector state and add raptorq flood regression

ObjectStoreCursorBackend previously loaded ConnectorStateObject
recovery records from the underlying ObjectStore and handed them to
the caller without cross-checking that the stored header, body, and
object id still matched the canonical form of the decoded state.
A mutation of the backing store (whether a bug in a store
implementation or an attacker with write access to a shared object
store) could therefore silently rewrite the header's refs list, drop
the lease reference, or swap bodies between records, and the SDK
would happily resume from the tampered state.

Introduce ObjectStoreCursorBackend::validate_loaded_state_object
which, for each load:

  - canonicalizes both the stored header and the header embedded in
    the decoded ConnectorStateObject via serde_json::to_vec and
    compares the bytes — any divergence raises
    CursorStoreError::Storage("stored connector state header/body
    mismatch").
  - requires state.header.refs to contain state.lease_object_id,
    preserving the invariant that recovery state references its own
    lease.
  - re-canonicalizes the decoded state through CanonicalSerializer
    and compares against stored.body.
  - re-derives the object id via StoredObject::derive_id using the
    backend's ObjectIdKey and compares against the actual object_id
    from the store.

CursorStoreBackend::load() now calls this validator; records that
fail the check are logged with tracing::warn and skipped
(continue in the iteration loop) rather than poisoning the caller.

A new test, cursor_store_object_store_rejects_tampered_recovery_
state, rewrites a stored ConnectorStateObject after commit and
asserts the backend refuses to surface the tampered record. Two of
the existing test construction sites are adjusted to use
Arc<dyn ObjectStore> so they type-check against the refined APIs.

crates/fcp-store/tests/store_repair_integration.rs gains the
adversarial_symbol_flood_is_capped_without_stale_decoder_state
regression: an attacker stream that floods more than the configured
max_symbols distinct ESIs at a decoder whose block never completes
must not buffer symbols past the cap, must not produce a stale
decoder state, and must return the expected
DecodeError::MemoryLimitExceeded once the cap is reached. This
exercises the new decode admission path that landed in the sibling
fcp-raptorq commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
